name: Nikto (Web Server Scanner)

auth.notes: Basic auth via -id user:pass; cookie injection supported

performance.notes: Sends thousands of requests; takes minutes to hours depending on host

agent_readiness.notes: Web server misconfiguration and vulnerability scanner. nikto -h https://target. Checks: outdated software, dangerous files, default credentials, server headers, SSL issues, 6700+ vulnerabilities. -Format xml/htm/csv/json for output. -Tuning (select check categories). -useproxy for Burp Suite integration. High false positive rate — manual verification required. Noisy (no evasion). Classic recon tool in OSCP/CTF. Part of Kali Linux default tools.

gotchas: Very high false positive rate — all findings need manual verification
Extremely noisy — detected by IDS/WAF immediately
Perl-based (not maintained as actively as modern alternatives)
best_when: Quick web server misconfiguration checks in authorized assessments
Identifying default files, dangerous HTTP methods, server banners
avoid_when: When OWASP ZAP provides better active scanning workflow
summary: Nikto is the classic web server vulnerability and misconfiguration scanner. Checks 6700+ issues: outdated software, dangerous files, default credentials, SSL problems. Noisy and high false positive rate — good for initial recon, not stealth assessments. Included in Kali Linux. Pairs with ffuf/gobuster for comprehensive web recon. Manual verification required for all findings.
